The problem asks us to arrange a series of benzene derivatives (1-6) in order of increasing weight fraction of the yield of the para-isomer when they undergo an electrophilic substitution reaction with an excess of bromine.

## 2: Identifying Directing Effects of Substituents
In electrophilic aromatic substitution (EAS), the directing effects of substituents on the benzene ring determine the position of the incoming electrophile. Substituents can be classified as ortho/para directors or meta directors. Ortho/para directors activate the ring and direct the electrophile to the ortho and para positions, while meta directors deactivate the ring and direct the electrophile to the meta positions.

## 3: Classifying Substituents
- **Ortho/Para Directors:** -CH3, -C2H5 (activating), -Cl (weakly activating but ortho/para directing due to resonance effect)
- **Meta Directors:** -NO2, -COOH, -COOC2H5 (deactivating)

## 4: Understanding the Effect on Para-Isomer Yield
The yield of the para-isomer is influenced by the directing effect of the substituent. Strong ortho/para directors tend to produce a higher fraction of para-isomer due to steric reasons (ortho positions are more sterically hindered). Meta directors produce less para-isomer and more meta-isomer.

## 5: Analyzing Substituents
1. **-CH3 (1):** Ortho/para director, moderate activation.
2. **-COOC2H5 (2):** Meta director, deactivation.
